title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police seek public's help identifying suspect in a January 22 armed robbery in Dorchester.
Robbery occurred around 4:30 p.m. near Adams and Whitten Streets and involved multiple victims.
Information can be reported to detectives at 617-343-4335 or anonymously via 1-800-494-TIPS.

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials demand $7.8 million security funding before issuing a license for World Cup games at Gillette Stadium.
Host committee lawyers say funds will be paid within two days post-games, but Foxborough insists on payment before March 17.
Massachusetts awaits $46 million in federal security funding; Governor Healey confident games will proceed as planned.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu to host inaugural office hours event at the Bruce C. Bolling Building in Roxbury on Thursday.
Residents can voice concerns about city services directly to Wu and city department staff during the two-hour event.
City services like voting registration and certificate issuance will be available at the event alongside department representatives.

title: 7 players Bruins could target at 2026 NHL trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney is seeking impactful players before the 2026 NHL trade deadline, focusing on long-term roster improvements.
Potential targets include Robert Thomas of St. Louis and Simon Nemec of New Jersey, both requiring significant trade assets.
St. Louis Blues' Justin Faulk and Philadelphia Flyers' Owen Tippett are considered for defensive and offensive upgrades, respectively.

title: Staff member at special needs residential program in Franklin accused of breaking autistic student’s arm
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Saturs Allison, 27, from Rhode Island, accused of breaking an autistic student's arm at a Franklin residential program.
Allison was indicted for assault on a disabled person and arraigned in Norfolk Superior Court, pleading not guilty.
Incident caught on surveillance; student required medical treatment and physical therapy after the alleged assault.

title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: 18-year-old Amanda Lewis is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her late friend Lily, who died of brain cancer.
Amanda, from Charlotte, North Carolina, is participating with Dana-Farber to raise money for cancer research and awareness.
Her journey is guided by the inspiration and emotional endurance developed through her grief and running experience.

title: Fall River man sentenced for raping woman when she refused to take naked pictures of young daughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Elias Rodrigues Dos Santos Mendes pleaded guilty to rape and child pornography charges in Fall River Superior Court.
Mendes sentenced to 8-10 years in prison for assaulting a woman who refused to take nude photos of her daughter.
DA's office states Mendes will be deported to Brazil after serving his sentence due to undocumented status.

title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots released Stefon Diggs to free up $17 million in cap space, needing a new WR1 for 2026.
New England could target free-agent Alec Pierce, who led the NFL in yards per catch with 1,003 yards in 2025.
Patriots may consider trading for receivers like A.J. Brown or drafting options if free agency targets fail.

title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brianna Poehler of Granby, Mass. will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Miles for Miracles supporting Boston Children's Hospital.
Poehler's daughter, a liver transplant survivor treated at Boston Children's, inspired her marathon participation.
Poehler aims to raise funds for research and care at Boston Children's Hospital through her marathon run.

title: Do you support the U.S. strikes on Iran? Here’s what 300 readers said.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Over 350 readers responded to a survey on U.S. strikes on Iran; 54% support, 43% oppose, and 3% are unsure.
Supporters cite Iran's funding of terrorism and describe its regime as oppressive, believing strikes may pressure change.
Opponents fear escalation into broader conflict and criticize lack of congressional approval and domestic focus diversion.
